name: Old-Time Country Ham with Redeye Gravy
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the ham slices in a large flat pan and cover with cold water. Let stand for 6-8 hours; drain and blot dry. Remove and discard the rind.
2. Fry the ham slices in a single layer in an ungreased heavy skillet over medium-high heat. Cook for 5-6 minutes on each side, then remove to a heated platter. Pour off all but about 2 tablespoons of fat.
3. Add boiling water and let boil up, scraping the bottom to pick up all flavorful bits of ham from the skillet. Stir in coffee.
4. Pour over the ham slices and serve at once.
